description: A formal document that authorizes the project, defines its objectives, identifies key stakeholders, and outlines the project manager's authority. This Project Charter will focus on the establishment of the residential research unit for adult NREM parasomnias, including the development of semi-automated event-triage tools. It serves as the foundation for all subsequent project planning and execution. Audience: Project Team, Stakeholders, University Administration.
steps_to_create: Define project goals and objectives based on the project plan.
Identify key stakeholders and their roles.
Outline project scope, deliverables, and success criteria.
Establish project governance and decision-making processes.
Obtain approval from relevant authorities (e.g., University Administration, Funding Agencies).

description: A comprehensive log of identified project risks, their potential impact, likelihood, and mitigation strategies. This Risk Register will specifically address risks related to regulatory approvals, technical failures, financial uncertainties, environmental challenges, social acceptance, and participant safety. Audience: Project Team, Stakeholders, External Scientific Advisory Board.
steps_to_create: Identify potential project risks based on the project plan and assumptions.
Assess the likelihood and impact of each risk.
Develop mitigation strategies for high-priority risks.
Assign responsibility for monitoring and managing each risk.
Regularly review and update the Risk Register throughout the project lifecycle.
approval_authorities: Principal Investigator, External Scientific Advisory Board

description: A detailed plan outlining how project information will be communicated to stakeholders, including frequency, channels, and responsible parties. This Communication Plan will address the needs of the project team, University Hospital Bonn, funding agencies, the external scientific advisory board, study participants, and the local community. Audience: Project Team, Stakeholders.
steps_to_create: Identify key stakeholders and their communication needs.
Define communication channels and frequency for each stakeholder group.
Assign responsibility for preparing and disseminating project information.
Establish a process for managing communication-related issues and feedback.
Regularly review and update the Communication Plan throughout the project lifecycle.

description: A plan outlining strategies for engaging and managing stakeholders throughout the project lifecycle. This Stakeholder Engagement Plan will focus on building positive relationships with the University Hospital Bonn, funding agencies, the external scientific advisory board, study participants, and the local community. Audience: Project Team, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_create: Identify key stakeholders and their interests, influence, and potential impact on the project.
Develop strategies for engaging and managing each stakeholder group.
Establish a process for addressing stakeholder concerns and feedback.
Regularly review and update the Stakeholder Engagement Plan throughout the project lifecycle.

description: A plan outlining the process for managing changes to the project scope, schedule, or budget. This Change Management Plan will address changes related to regulatory requirements, technical challenges, financial constraints, and stakeholder feedback. Audience: Project Team, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_create: Establish a process for identifying, documenting, and evaluating change requests.
Define the roles and responsibilities for reviewing and approving change requests.
Develop a process for communicating approved changes to stakeholders.
Track and manage the implementation of approved changes.
Regularly review and update the Change Management Plan throughout the project lifecycle.

description: A high-level overview of the project budget, including funding sources, allocation of funds across project phases, and contingency planning. This framework will outline the budget for personnel, equipment, facility renovation, participant compensation, data analysis software, and publication fees. Audience: Project Team, University Administration, Funding Agencies.
steps_to_create: Develop a detailed work breakdown structure (WBS) for the project.
Estimate the cost of each task in the WBS.
Allocate funds to each task based on its priority and resource requirements.
Identify potential funding sources and secure commitments.
Establish a process for monitoring and controlling project costs.
Conduct a sensitivity analysis to assess the impact of potential cost increases.

description: A template outlining the structure and terms of funding agreements with potential pharmaceutical partners or other external funding sources. This template will address intellectual property rights, data sharing agreements, and reporting requirements. Audience: Principal Investigator, University Administration, Legal Counsel.
steps_to_create: Consult with legal counsel to develop a standard funding agreement template.
Define the terms and conditions for funding, including intellectual property rights and data sharing.
Establish reporting requirements and milestones for funding disbursement.
Obtain approval from University Administration and Legal Counsel.

description: A high-level timeline outlining the key project milestones and deliverables, including facility setup, equipment procurement, participant recruitment, data collection, data analysis, and publication. This timeline will serve as a roadmap for project execution and will be regularly updated to reflect progress and changes. Audience: Project Team, Stakeholders.
steps_to_create: Identify key project milestones and deliverables.
Estimate the duration of each task.
Sequence the tasks and create a project timeline.
Assign responsibility for completing each task.
Regularly review and update the timeline throughout the project lifecycle.

description: A framework for monitoring and evaluating the project's progress and impact, including key performance indicators (KPIs), data collection methods, and reporting frequency. This framework will track the number of participants recruited, the number of publications, the amount of grant funding secured, and the impact of research findings. Audience: Project Team, External Scientific Advisory Board, Funding Agencies.
steps_to_create: Define key performance indicators (KPIs) for each project objective.
Establish data collection methods for tracking KPIs.
Define reporting frequency and formats.
Assign responsibility for monitoring and evaluating project progress.
Regularly review and update the M&E framework throughout the project lifecycle.
approval_authorities: External Scientific Advisory Board, Funding Agencies
